---
title: "Vocabulary Norms"
---

Vocabulary size as a function of age. Curves show empirical quantiles
(10th/25th/50th/75th/90th percentile) of total vocabulary among children in
the Wordbank database, per instrument.

```{r load-vocab}
suppressMessages(library(dplyr))
if (file.exists("data/vocab_summaries.parquet")) {
  vocab_summaries <- arrow::read_parquet("data/vocab_summaries.parquet")
} else {
  library(redivis)
  vocab_summaries <- redivis$organization("datapages")$dataset("wordbank")$
    table("vocab_summaries")$to_tibble()
}
ojs_define(vocab = vocab_summaries)
```

```{ojs setup-norms}
//| output: false
Plot = import("https://esm.sh/@observablehq/plot@0.6.17")

v = transpose(vocab)
norm_languages = [...new Set(v.map((d) => d.language))].sort()
```

::::{.panel-input}

```{ojs inputs-norms}
viewof nlanguage = Inputs.select(norm_languages, {
  label: "Language",
  value: "English (American)"
})
viewof nform = Inputs.select(
  [...new Set(v.filter((d) => d.language === nlanguage).map((d) => d.form))],
  { label: "Form" }
)
viewof nmeasure = Inputs.select(
  [...new Set(v.filter((d) => d.language === nlanguage && d.form === nform)
    .map((d) => d.measure))],
  { label: "Measure" }
)
```

::::

```{ojs norms-data}
//| output: false
norms_data = v.filter((d) =>
  d.language === nlanguage && d.form === nform && d.measure === nmeasure)
```

```{ojs norms-plot}
Plot.plot({
  style: { fontFamily: "var(--sans-serif)", fontSize: "13px" },
  width: 900,
  height: 500,
  inset: 8,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "Age (months)", tickFormat: "" },
  y: { label: `${nmeasure === "production" ? "Productive" : "Receptive"} vocabulary (words)` },
  color: { legend: true, type: "ordinal", scheme: "viridis", label: "Quantile" },
  marks: [
    Plot.lineY(norms_data, Plot.windowY(
      { k: 3, reduce: "mean" },
      { x: "age", y: "vocab", stroke: (d) => `${Math.round(d.quantile * 100)}th`,
        strokeWidth: 2.5, curve: "basis" }
    )),
    Plot.dot(norms_data, {
      x: "age", y: "vocab",
      fill: (d) => `${Math.round(d.quantile * 100)}th`,
      r: 2.5, fillOpacity: 0.6,
      channels: { n_children: "n_children" },
      tip: true
    })
  ]
})
```

::: {.callout-note}
These are empirical quantiles of the archive's convenience sample, smoothed
over adjacent ages — a useful descriptive picture, but not clinical norms.
For norming applications see the [official CDI norms](https://mb-cdi.stanford.edu/).
:::
